title: Closed-Book vs. Open-Book QA
concept: Open-book QA searches a database for context before answering; closed-book QA relies entirely on the model's memorized weights. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Machine Translation.'

exercises.prompt: What does an 'open-book' Question Answering (QA) system do?

options.text: It only works when reading digital PDF novels.
It searches a database for information before answering.
It requires the user to read a book to the AI.

exercises.explain: Open-book QA is like taking an exam with your notes open—you search for the facts before writing down the answer.

cards.title: To Search or to Guess?
cards.body: Imagine answering a trivia question about a brand-new movie. If you haven't seen it, you can only guess based on what you already know. But what if you could quickly look up the movie's Wiki page first? That's the difference between closed-book and open-book AI!

cards.title: What is Book-style QA?
cards.body:
**Closed-book QA** models answer questions using only the information memorized in their weights during training. 

**Open-book QA** models first search an external database or the internet to find relevant documents, then read those documents to write a precise answer.

cards.title: The Exam Day Analogy
cards.body:
Think of a history exam. 

A **closed-book** student must answer everything using only their memory. 

An **open-book** student gets to search through a giant stack of textbooks right there at their desk to find the exact facts before writing their answer.

cards.title: In Action
cards.body:
If you ask: *"Who won the marathon in 2023?"*

• **Closed-book QA** answers instantly from memory (but might get it wrong if it hasn't been updated since 2022).
• **Open-book QA** searches the web for "2023 marathon winner," finds a news article, and uses that article to give you the correct, up-to-date name.

cards.title: Does Bigger Mean Smarter?
cards.body:
**Myth:** Closed-book models are always better because they don't need a database.
**Fact:** While closed-book models are faster, they easily hallucinate (make up facts) and cannot access new information that wasn't in their original training data. Open-book systems stay updated easily!

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Let's compare how both styles handle a question. One relies entirely on internal memory, while the other fetches external proof first.

left.text: User Question ➔ AI Model Memory ➔ Answer (Fast, but might hallucinate outdated facts)

right.text: User Question ➔ Database Search ➔ Relevant Text ➔ AI Model ➔ Answer (Accurate and verified)

exercises.prompt: What makes an AI system an 'open-book' QA system?

options.text: It retrieves external documents first to find the answer.
It closes all databases to save computer memory.
It translates the question into multiple languages.
It only answers questions about physical paper books.

exercises.explain: Open-book QA searches a database or documents to retrieve facts before generating an answer, keeping it accurate and up-to-date.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Open-book QA makes it easier to update the AI's knowledge without retraining the whole model.
exercises.explain: Open-book systems can simply search a updated database, meaning you don't need to spend days retraining the core AI model just to teach it new facts.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how closed-book QA works.
exercises.sentence: Closed-book QA models answer questions using only their memorized internal [blank].
exercises.explain: Closed-book models cannot look up new files or websites; they must rely entirely on what is stored inside their internal weights.

exercises.prompt: Identify which of these is a Closed-Book QA system.

options.text: System A: A search engine that finds web pages.
System B: An AI model answering purely from its training memory.
System C: An AI assistant that searches a folder of PDFs first.

exercises.explain: System B is an AI model acting alone (closed-book). Systems A and C represent open-book systems because they search and retrieve external context before answering.

exercises.prompt: You are building a customer support chatbot that needs to look up real-time shipping policies from your company database. Which approach should you choose and why?

options.text: Open-book QA uses less memory because it doesn't need to search any database.
Open-book QA searches external documents for context, while closed-book QA relies only on memorized weights.
Closed-book QA is always more accurate because it cannot hallucinate facts.
Both methods require the model to access the live internet for every single query.

exercises.explain: Closed-book QA relies purely on what the model learned during training, whereas open-book QA searches an external database to grab fresh, accurate context before answering. Next up: Machine Translation.
